sentences.text: When someone signs up, their account is created in the Unverified state.
We email a verification code, and when the user enters it correctly the account becomes Active.
If the code is entered incorrectly three times, the account moves to Locked.
A support agent can unlock a Locked account, returning it to Unverified.
An Active account can be deactivated by its owner, moving it to Deactivated.

rankedHoles.rationale: Unlocking an account that is not locked should have an explicit outcome.
The spec should say whether an unverified account can be deactivated.
A verification code entered after activation may be safely ignored.
The spec should define whether failed verification attempts can lock an active account.
Unlocking an already active account should have an idempotent outcome.
The spec should define whether a correct code can recover a locked account.
Further incorrect entries while locked should have an explicit no-op outcome.
Owners or support may need to deactivate a locked account.

suggestedEvents.name: Verification code expired
suggestedEvents.rationale: Verification codes normally expire and require a new-code path.
